pr.title: Match some more gm_1601 functions, and fix data sections
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Matched a large batch of gm_1601 result/menu/game-mode helper functions and corrected nearby rodata/data/sdata symbol boundaries and data typing. The PR added explicit character-name SJIS tables, language-aware name lookup helpers, unlockable-character lookup tables, audio/result helpers, clearer function prototypes, and static data definitions that replaced overly broad or zero-filled placeholder data. decomp.dev reported +4336 matched bytes, 36 new matches, and 3 improved unmatched functions, all in main/melee/gm/gm_1601.
what_changed: Added JP and US character-name SJIS string tables in gm_1601.c, plus special-case reduced name tables for Fox and Samus, and used lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS for language-dependent result text.
Reworked result-animation and victory-theme tables to use CharacterKind/CHKIND_NONE-style values rather than raw magic numbers where possible.

Split config/GALE01/symbols.txt rodata labels around 0x803B75F8 from broad untyped blobs into separate float, 2-byte, byte, and 4-byte data objects, including lbl_803B75F8, lbl_803B767C, lbl_803B7700, lbl_803B7784, lbl_803B7978, lbl_803B79BC, and lbl_803B7A00.
Marked many gmMainLib data/sdata labels in symbols.txt as data:sjis, and adjusted sizes where evidence showed string/SJIS boundaries such as lbl_803D4C38 size 0xF and lbl_803D51A0 as data:byte.

Moved and filled static rodata definitions in gm_1601.static.h, including float scale arrays, s16 trophy/result tables, GXColor table lbl_803B7864, unlockable-character table lbl_803B78C8, lbl_803B790C, and Vec2 data lbl_803B7930.
Refined struct lbl_803B78C8_0x6 fields from inferred padding to idx, ckind, x2, x3, and x4, allowing cleaner unlockable-character lookup code.
Expanded struct lbl_8046B488_t with an x1B8 function pointer while preserving the 0x1E0 static assert.

Updated gm_1601.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or broad int/s32 forms to more specific return types and arguments such as CharacterKind, MajorSceneKind, bool, u8, const char*, HSD_Text*, and MatchEnd*.
Changed GetPersistentNameData to return struct gmm_x2FF8_inner* instead of void*, and updated callers to use typed persistent-name data rather than local anonymous padding structs.
Adjusted related function declarations/usages across gmmain_lib, gm_1B03, gmresultplayer, mnsoundtest, grstadium, player, lbaudio_ax, and sislib to fit the improved gm data/types.
smart_moves: Fixed matching by correcting data-section boundaries first: the old broad rodata object at lbl_803B75F8 was split into independently typed arrays, which made functions naturally index float, s16, byte, GXColor, and Vec2 tables instead of accessing artificial struct fields.
Used actual language state via lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS rather than hardcoding one regional table, enabling result-name functions to match around JP/US SJIS strings and per-language scale factors.
Kept sentinel-based table walking but replaced raw 0x21 checks with CHKIND_NONE in result/victory lookup code, improving readability without changing the table-driven shape.
Introduced a small static inline helper fn_801606A8_inline for the unlockable-character conversion path, preserving codegen while avoiding duplicated table scan logic.
Preserved known layout guarantees with STATIC_ASSERTs while refining struct internals, especially for lbl_8046B488_t and the persistent-name data structs.
Used typed fields in struct gmm_x2FF8_inner and GetPersistentNameData to remove anonymous padding casts in consumers such as gm_1B03.c.
decomp_lessons: When many functions near a rodata block refuse to match, audit the symbol map: one oversized object can hide multiple adjacent arrays with distinct element types and prevent natural C from matching.
SJIS strings in .data/.sdata should be annotated as data:sjis in symbols.txt when known; this improves searchability and prevents ambiguous untyped object treatment.
For table-driven character logic, prefer CharacterKind names and explicit sentinel constants over raw character IDs once the table contents are known.

Do not keep placeholder aggregate structs like gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t if the binary evidence supports separate arrays; split them into the real data objects so accesses compile as simple indexed loads.

Precise header prototypes matter for matching. Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with bool/u8/int/const char*/typed pointer signatures can remove casts, reveal correct signedness, and help downstream callers match.
Language-specific UI text often couples string tables with per-character scale tables; matching text draw functions may require reconstructing both the string selection and scale-factor arrays.
Typed persistent save/name data is reusable across gm modules: once GetPersistentNameData has a concrete return type, callers can access fields directly and avoid local fake structs.
naming_conventions: Uses CharacterKind parameter names such as ckind for character-kind values.
Uses CHKIND_NONE as the sentinel in character/result tables instead of raw 0x21 where evidence allowed.
Keeps unknown global labels as address-based lbl_803Bxxxx/lbl_803Dxxxx names, but documents obvious tables with comments such as JP character names and US character names.
Refines inferred struct field names only when usage is clear, e.g. lbl_803B78C8_0x6 fields idx and ckind while leaving less-certain fields as x2, x3, x4.
Uses function-address names gm_8016xxxx/fn_8016xxxx where semantic names are not yet known, but improves parameter names like port, slot, costume_id, text, and item_mask.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a broad placeholder data struct and replaced it with exact static arrays whose element types match load instructions: float arrays for text scale, s16 arrays for indexes, byte arrays for IDs, GXColor for color entries, and Vec2 for coordinate pairs.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around gm_801603B0 instead of a wider dont_inline reset region, localizing the inlining constraint.
Adjusted floating-point expression shape in gm_801601C4/gm_80160244 by splitting computation into division then multiplication before comparison, matching generated instruction order.
Simplified gm_801602C0 by calling gm_80160244 and gm_801601C4 and retaining a PAD_STACK(8), indicating stack/codegen sensitivity remained.
Matched table scans with explicit for loops over NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS and direct indexed array access rather than pointer iteration where that matched better.
Used switch statements with explicit lbAudioAx_* calls for sound-effect dispatch in gm_80168C5C and fn_80168E54.
Changed return and argument signedness in headers and definitions, for example fn_80167194 from u8 to int and rumble/name-data arguments from s8 to int, to align callers and generated code.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 40.98%, +0.11%, +4336 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported 36 new matches in main/melee/gm/gm_1601 and 3 improvements in unmatched functions: gm_80160B40 to 95.24%, gm_80164840 to 99.42%, and gm_80167BC8 to 92.30%.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish gm_80160B40, gm_80164840, or gm_80167BC8 after this PR left them below 100%.
Search for additional gm_1601 data-section cleanup around lbl_803D4FDC, lbl_803D5060, lbl_803D50E4, and result-screen SJIS tables.
Search for future renames of lbl_803B78C8_0x6, lbl_803D5168_t, and lbl_803D51A0 to learn final semantic names.
Search for subsequent changes to GetPersistentNameData and struct gmm_x2FF8_inner to see how persistent-name data layout evolved.
